intro: Pigafetta malabarica, also known as Indian gentian or Malabar gentian, is a plant species that belongs to the Gentianaceae family, characterized by its distinctive yellow flowers and medicinal properties. Traditionally, Pigafetta malabarica has been used in Ayurvedic medicine to treat various health conditions, including fever, respiratory issues, and digestive problems, due to its antimicrobial, anti-inflammatory, and antioxidant properties. The bioactive constituents of Pigafetta malabarica include iridoid glycosides, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In herbal medicine, Pigafetta malabarica is often prepared as a decoction or a tincture, and is also used in traditional remedies such as teas, infusions, and capsules.
